papers.name: Paper 1
papers.coverage_notes: Compulsory part (4 topics). Source: C&A Guide p.80.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.coverage_notes: Elective part; students answer on 2 of the 4 elective topics. Source: C&A Guide p.80.

papers.name: School-based Assessment
papers.coverage_notes: Practical and inquiry skills assessed by school teachers, moderated by HKEAA. Source: C&A Guide p.80-81.

assessment_objectives.name: Knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Recall and understand biological facts, concepts, principles and interrelationships among topics.
classification_hints: Items asking for definitions, structures, processes, terminology

assessment_objectives.name: Scientific investigation and practical skills
assessment_objectives.description: Formulate hypotheses, design and carry out experiments, make observations, handle apparatus safely, and report investigations.
classification_hints: Items involving experimental design, apparatus, variables, safety, practical procedures

assessment_objectives.name: Application and data analysis
assessment_objectives.description: Apply biological knowledge to familiar and unfamiliar situations; process, interpret and translate data and non-data material.
classification_hints: Items requiring graph/diagram interpretation, calculation, application to novel contexts

assessment_objectives.name: Evaluation and communication
assessment_objectives.description: Evaluate evidence, identify errors, propose original ideas, synthesise information, and communicate logically; consider STSE implications.
classification_hints: Essay-style or evaluation questions; ethical, social, environmental arguments

question_styles.name: Multiple choice
question_styles.description: Broad coverage of the curriculum; tests recall and basic application.

question_styles.name: Short answer
question_styles.description: Tests basic knowledge and concepts in concise responses.

question_styles.name: Structured question
question_styles.description: Requires analysis of provided information and application to different contexts.

question_styles.name: Essay
question_styles.description: In-depth discussion of biology-related issues; tests logical organisation and expression.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or direct one-step application within a single topic.
Multi-step application within one topic; interpret simple data or diagrams.
Cross-topic synthesis, unfamiliar contexts, evaluation or extended explanation.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when at least one teacher/student/parent-facing mode is intentionally declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: DSE Biology command terms are implied in the C&A Guide and annual examination reports; no separate command-word appendix is in this source.
marking_guidance.notes: Marks are awarded for correct biological knowledge, application, and communication. Partial credit is common for multi-step structured questions.
